specific_mechanism: PH-Memory: store per-episode persistence diagram + bottleneck-distance retrieval. Per episode, build entity-relation simplicial complex; filter by edge weights; compute PD_E in dim 0,1,2. Memory key = PD_E. At query Q, compute PD_Q; retrieve top-k episodes by minimizing d_B(PD_Q, PD_E).
llm_application: PH-Memory: maintain a memory bank of {(PD_E, raw_text_E)} pairs. For each new episode, extract entity-relation graph G_E, build Vietoris-Rips filtration over edge-weight scan, compute PD_E using Gudhi/Ripser. Store. At query time, build G_Q, compute PD_Q, retrieve via top-k bottleneck distance d_B(PD_Q, PD_E). Combine with text-embedding similarity via weighted sum.
novelty_claim: PH-NLP exists (AwesomeTDA4NLP collection), PH for personalized search and curiosity prediction documented. Episodic-memory LLM exists (arXiv 2502.06975, 2601.21714 E-mem). PH-as-episodic-memory-key specifically is less common.
motivation_justification: PH is a closed-form algebraic invariant of filtered simplicial complexes. Bottleneck distance has explicit formula. Direct mechanism transfer.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: Entity-relation simplicial complex per episode
content_words: topological memory key store rank top-k
long-context memory bank persistent topological invariant
